D6 Display L4 Analyze Reference

OLED Emission Spectrum

three organic emitters glow in broad bands — their sum is the white, and per-pixel emission means true blacks

An OLED makes light at the pixel — no backlight — so a black pixel is simply off, giving near-infinite contrast. Each colour comes from an organic emitter with a fairly broad band (tens of nanometres wide). The three bands add to white; their balance sets the colour temperature.

The red, green, and blue emission bands are plotted with their sum. Slide the blue level to warm or cool the white. The bands are wide compared with quantum dots — which is why OLED gamut, though good, trails QD-LED.
